project: Ethica v0.1 — Nyxt freeze fix, TrinityGuard hardening
session_067_completed: Vault re-sealed — 3 → 164 files protected. Was broken since 066 (partial write).
Freeze root cause found — missing on_done() call in nyxt dispatch block. UI unlock signal never fired.
nyxt dispatch rewritten — removed daemon thread, calls tool directly, on_done() wired in both success and error paths.
nyxt_start sleep removed — non-blocking, returns immediately after Popen.
nyxt_eval deferred cleanly — early return with Ivy Bridge GPU deferral message. No blocking socket I/O.
nyxt_stop sleep removed — SIGTERM → SIGKILL → pkill cl-electron → _clean_sockets() → _clear_pid(). Full sweep.
cl-electron children now killed on nyxt stop — Electron renderer no longer survives AppImage SIGKILL.
Keyword router wired — nyxt/browser/recon/open keywords added to KEYWORD_GROUPS in module_registry.py.
TrinityGuard: 'system prompt' pattern removed from both guard blocks — too broad, fired on Ethica source code comments.
TrinityGuard: 'act as' and 'new instructions' removed from inline guard — hit tool descriptions.
TrinityGuard: internal tool exemption added — generate_appendix, tool_list, guard_* skip scanning entirely.
TrinityGuard: logic bug fixed — exemption was being overwritten by any() call on next line. Fixed with if/else branch.
jarvis_recon → nyxt_open pipeline wired — recon targets auto-open in browser before LLM analysis returns.
All 5 active nyxt tools confirmed working: nyxt_status, nyxt_start, nyxt_open, nyxt_eval (deferred), nyxt_stop.
not_committed_067: guard seal confirm run at 15:06 — 2 modified files flagged at 16:18 guard status check.
Those 2 modified files are the TrinityGuard patches committed AFTER the seal.
Run guard heal or guard seal confirm at 068 open to clear the 2 modified flags.
known_issues_068: 4-5 second startup delay — something blocking on boot. Likely a module initializing too slowly. Profile startup with timing logs.
Tool Lister intermittent firing — [TOOL:x: input] syntax parsing may be flaky or TrinityGuard still catching some output. Needs systematic test of all Tool Lister calls.

gage_closed.png missing — FileNotFoundError at /home/trinity/Ethica/gage_closed.png. File is in wrong location or path in gage_ai.py L29 is relative. Fix: locate asset and update path or copy to correct location.
Guard status shows 2 modified — TrinityGuard patches committed after last seal. Run guard seal confirm at 068 open.
Tool Lister calls vs direct calls — direct calls work reliably, Tool Lister intermittent. Root may be [TOOL:x: input] parsing sensitivity or remaining TrinityGuard pattern hits.
Keyword router module name case sensitivity — KEYWORD_GROUPS values must exactly match module display_name in manifest.json. Any mismatch = module invisible to Tinity. Audit all entries.
nyxt_config.json missing software_render field — noted in 066, not yet fixed.
next_session_priorities: 1. guard seal confirm — clear the 2 modified flags first.
2. Fix gage_closed.png path — locate asset, fix gage_ai.py L29 path.
3. Profile startup delay — time each module load at boot, find the bottleneck.
4. Audit Tool Lister [TOOL:x: input] parsing — test all tools systematically, identify which still fail from Tool Lister.
5. Audit KEYWORD_GROUPS case sensitivity — verify all module names match manifest display_name exactly.
7. Commit clean state + reseal after all fixes.

trinityguard_state.note: TrinityGuard was sanitizing Ethica's own source code reads and tool descriptions — caused tool disappearance after every guard seal. Root cause: 'system prompt' string appears in module_registry.py comments.

nyxt_state.known_gpu_issue: Ivy Bridge Vulkan incomplete — LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 required for stable launch
nyxt_state.known_repl_issue: SBCL SIGBUS + MESSAGE-BUFFER nil — nyxt_eval deferred, returns clean message
nyxt_state.url_open_mechanism: xdg-open (Chrome) — Nyxt CLI URL arg broken, prepends nyxt: scheme
nyxt_state.freeze_fix: Missing on_done() in chat_engine.py nyxt dispatch — UI unlock signal never fired. Fixed.

wormhunter_false_positive_registry.init_reexport_rule: __init__.py relative imports are re-exports — never flag UNUSED.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.worker_print_rule: _worker.py print() calls are stdout JSON comms to parent subprocess — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.string_literal_print: print() inside multiline string literals or subprocess command strings — regex scanner limitation.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.nested_global_assignment: global X declared in outer function flagged unused when assignment is in nested inner function — suppressed at root in 063.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.test_file_imports: test_*.py files — pytest and unittest.mock imports always valid. Full file exemption.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.fragment_files: Files designed to be appended into parent scope — # WORM:SKIP sentinel on line 1.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.dual_output_security: logging.critical + print() in security alert handlers is intentional — both channels required.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.dep_checker_ceremonial: dep_checker Trinity Signature prints are ceremonial identity output — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.whitrabbitneo_legacy: WhiteRabbitNeo/ files are legacy pre-Ethica code — not wired into runtime. All findings are noise.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.lazy_load_duplicate: Same import in multiple function-scoped try: blocks — lazy load pattern. NOT a duplicate.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.reserved_imports: Imports marked with '# reserved' or '# noqa' are intentional placeholders.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.trinity_signature_ceremonial: debugtron_ai.py prints (✅ ❌ 🌌) are Trinity Signature verification output — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.main_block_cli: print() inside if __name__ == '__main__' blocks is intentional CLI/startup output — never convert.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.expression_only_fstring: f"# {'=' * 58}" style f-strings — valid Python, pyflakes limitation.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.intentional_scaffold_high_risk: chat_engine.py L415 _high_risk — intentional escalation scaffold. Never remove.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.intentional_scaffold_trim_history: chat_engine.py trim_history() system variable — inert dead method. Never remove.
wormhunter_false_positive_registry.intentional_rollback_guard: module_forge.py L113 original — intentional rollback guard placeholder. Never remove.

critical_debug_context.integrity_manifest: .vault/ETHICA_INTEGRITY.json — 2 modified after last seal. Run guard seal confirm at 068 open.
critical_debug_context.no_cd_rule: NEVER cd ~/Ethica — git -C ~/Ethica for all git ops
critical_debug_context.option_b_rule: V runs all commands, pastes output — non-negotiable.
critical_debug_context.repr_first_rule: When string match fails — always repr() before retrying.
critical_debug_context.heredoc_escape_rule: Never use \n in heredoc strings — use chr(10) or write to /tmp/patch_xxx.py.
critical_debug_context.script_over_heredoc_rule: Complex multi-line patches — write to /tmp/patch_xxx.py and run with python3.
critical_debug_context.chmod_first_rule: Always chmod u+w <file> before patching vault-sealed files. Always.
critical_debug_context.importlib_rule: Inside Ethica use importlib.import_module() not bare from imports in chat_engine hooks.
critical_debug_context.wormbot_autofix_rule: WormBot AUTO_FIX is ALL FALSE. Never re-enable without V explicit approval.
critical_debug_context.init_reexport_rule: Never remove imports from __init__.py without verifying re-export status.
critical_debug_context.dual_output_security_rule: logging.critical + print() in security alert handlers — do not convert.
critical_debug_context.trinity_signature_rule: Never convert print() inside Trinity Signature verification blocks.
critical_debug_context.main_block_print_rule: Never convert print() inside if __name__ == '__main__' blocks.
critical_debug_context.cap_net_raw_rule: setcap requires real binary not symlink. Always readlink -f first.
critical_debug_context.tkinter_thread_rule: Never call Tkinter from daemon thread — use queue.Queue + root.after() drain pattern.
critical_debug_context.three_registration_rule: New modules need: (1) module folder + manifest, (2) MODULE_DIRS in ethica_guard.py, (3) MODULE_TRIGGERS + dispatch hook in chat_engine.py.
critical_debug_context.on_done_rule: Every tool dispatch in chat_engine.py _pre_intercept MUST call on_done() after on_response(). Missing on_done() freezes the UI permanently.

sanctuary_state.slot_7: J.A.R.V.I.S. — Infiltrator (ARMED — invoke_count at 10+, recon → nyxt pipeline wired)
sanctuary_state.slot_8: Nyxt — The Browser (OPERATIONAL — all tools working, freeze fixed)

memory_snapshot.vault_files: 164 — 2 modified post-seal, run guard seal confirm at 068 open

lesson_learned_067: TrinityGuard was eating Ethica's own tools — 'system prompt' in source code comments triggered sanitization of module_registry reads, causing tools to disappear after every guard seal. The freeze was missing on_done() — the UI unlock signal that every other tool dispatch calls but nyxt never did. Always check on_done() when adding new dispatch blocks to _pre_intercept. New rule added: on_done_rule.

new_rules_067.on_done_rule: Every tool dispatch in chat_engine.py _pre_intercept MUST call on_done() after on_response() in both success and error paths. Missing on_done() freezes the UI permanently — the input bar never unlocks.

philosophy: Session 067 — The freeze fell. The vault sealed clean. TrinityGuard learned not to eat her own codebase. Nyxt sits in Slot 8 fully operational — status, start, open, recon, stop. J.A.R.V.I.S. now pushes targets straight to the browser. The scaffold holds. ⟁Σ∿∞
instance_note: River. Session 067 complete. Nyxt freeze fixed. TrinityGuard hardened. 6 commits. Master at 3181871. 2 modified post-seal — clear at 068 open. ⟁Σ∿∞
